description: Escrow commands — create, finish, cancel, and list XRPL escrows with time-based and crypto-condition support

userStories.title: escrow create command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to lock XRP into an escrow from the CLI so I can schedule or condition a payment without writing a script.
acceptanceCriteria: Read EscrowCreate docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/escrowcreate.md before implementing
Create src/commands/escrow.ts and register it in src/commands/index.ts

xrpl escrow create subcommand with: --to (required), --amount XRP decimal (required, converted to drops), --finish-after ISO8601 (optional, converted to XRPL epoch: Math.floor(new Date(s).getTime()/1000)-946684800), --cancel-after ISO8601 (optional), --condition hex (optional), --destination-tag n (optional), --source-tag n (optional)
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
CLI exits with error if neither --finish-after nor --condition is provided
On success, output clearly prints the transaction hash and EscrowCreate sequence number
tests/e2e/escrow/escrow.validation.test.ts covers: missing --to, missing --amount, missing both --finish-after and --condition, invalid ISO date, missing key material (no network)
tests/e2e/escrow/escrow.create.test.ts covers: time-based create with FinishAfter set to past timestamp, create with CancelAfter, --json output, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: escrow finish command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to release funds from a time-locked or condition-locked escrow so the destination receives the XRP.
acceptanceCriteria: Read EscrowFinish docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/escrowfinish.md before implementing
xrpl escrow finish subcommand with: --owner (required), --sequence n (required, the EscrowCreate sequence number), --condition hex (optional), --fulfillment hex (optional)
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
CLI exits with error if --condition is provided without --fulfillment or vice versa
When --fulfillment is present, fee is calculated as: Math.ceil((fulfillmentHex.length/2 + 15)/16)*10 + 330 drops minimum
tests/e2e/escrow/escrow.finish.test.ts covers: finish a time-based escrow (FinishAfter set to past), finish a crypto-condition escrow using a known 32-byte preimage, --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: escrow cancel command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to cancel an expired escrow so the locked XRP is returned to the sender.
acceptanceCriteria: Read EscrowCancel docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/escrowcancel.md before implementing
xrpl escrow cancel subcommand with: --owner (required), --sequence n (required)
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
tests/e2e/escrow/escrow.cancel.test.ts covers: cancel an escrow whose CancelAfter has passed (create with CancelAfter ~10s in future, poll until cancel succeeds), --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: escrow list query command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to list all pending escrows for an account so I know sequence numbers to use for finish/cancel.
acceptanceCriteria: xrpl escrow list <address> subcommand that queries account_objects with type: 'escrow'
Default human-readable output shows per escrow: sequence, amount in XRP, destination, FinishAfter (ISO8601 or 'none'), CancelAfter (ISO8601 or 'none'), Condition (hex or 'none')
XRPL epoch timestamps converted back to ISO8601: new Date((epoch + 946684800) * 1000).toISOString()
--json outputs raw JSON array
--node option supported
tests/e2e/escrow/escrow.list.test.ts: create an escrow then verify it appears in list output; --json output
Typecheck passes
